t: MoCA, MMSE, neuropsychological (visuospatial and executive function dominant).
Brain MRI (hippocampal atrophy less than in Alzheimer's).
Dementia occurring ≥ 1 year after parkinsonism (1-year rule)
In DLB particularly effective (cortical acetylcholine reduction stronger than in Alzheimer); rivastigmine McKeith I. Lancet 2000 RCT.
Use levodopa with caution; it can intensify psychotic symptoms.
RBD — melatonin (3–12 mg at night) or clonazepam (low dose).
FDA antipsychotic black box in elderly dementia patients.
McKeith I. Lancet 2000 — rivastigmine in DLB.
NICE NG97 — rivastigmine or donepezil for DLB.
In DLB, specifically effective; patch and oral.
Low dose; clozapine is the safest but requires monitoring.
Moderate expected lifespan from diagnosis 5–8 years; sometimes shorter than Alzheimer's.
Antipsychotic reaction is a critical complication.
Myth 1: "DLB is only a subtype of Alzheimer's"
distinct pathological and clinical unit; α-synuclein vs amyloid-β/tau; antipsychotic sensitivity is critical difference.
Haloperidol and risperidone are EXTRAORDINARY — neuroleptic malignant syndrome-like reaction, mortality risk high.
Some patients are tolerant to hallucinations; different approach if no distress.
Myth 4: "Levodopa fully cures parkinsonism in DLB"
Levodopa response in Parkinson's disease may be less; it can exacerbate psychotic symptoms; balance is required.
RBD is a prodrome of α-synucleinopathy (DLB, Parkinson's, MSA) — future development in 80%+ within 10-15 years.
WHO. ICD-11. 6D82 Dementia due to Lewy body disease. 2024.
McKeith I.G. et al. Diagnosis and management of dementia with Lewy bodies: Fourth consensus report. Neurology 2017;89(1):88–100.
McKeith I. et al. Efficacy of rivastigmine in dementia with Lewy bodies: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled international study. Lancet 2000;356(9247):2031–2036.
